title: cyathea decurrens
intro: Cyathea decurrens, also known as the Rough Tree Fern, is a species of tree fern native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing coughs and relieving respiratory issues, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Cyathea decurrens can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
